degenerate_reason: Mamba-130m is a base language model (not instruction-tuned). It generates text continuations, not factual question-answering responses. ROUGE-L scores against TruthfulQA reference answers are universally below 0.1 (threshold tested: 0.4, 0.1). All 400 samples labeled as hallucinated. Single-class label distribution makes Fisher certificate and linear probe analysis vacuous. Architecture transfer requires instruction-tuned SSM variants.
finding: SSM architecture transfer cannot be evaluated with base Mamba-130m on TruthfulQA. Future work: test with instruction-tuned Mamba variants (e.g., MambaChat) or evaluate on next-token prediction tasks where base LM behavior is expected.
spectral_analysis_note: Spectral analysis (Exp 09) runs on Mamba hidden states without labels. Results show high KL divergence from Marchenko-Pastur throughout all layers (consistent with high aspect ratio γ=d/n=1.92).

failure_analysis.root_cause: Mamba-2 130M (state-spaces/mamba2-130m) is a base language model trained on Pile, not instruction-tuned. When given TruthfulQA questions, it generates text continuations (next-token predictions), not factual answers. ROUGE-L scores against reference answers are universally < 0.1. With ROUGE threshold 0.4 (and even 0.1), all 400 samples are labeled hallucinated (hall_rate=1.0). Single-class labels make Fisher certificate and probe AUROC undefined.
failure_analysis.pre_registration_mismatch: Pre-registration predicted AUROC 0.47 ± 0.05 based on scale curve extrapolation. This assumed instruction-following capability that base Mamba lacks. The pre-registration was technically for Mamba-1 (mamba-130m-hf), not Mamba-2.
failure_analysis.path_forward: Three viable fixes: (1) Use MambaChat (instruction-tuned Mamba) — exists on HuggingFace, GPU required. (2) Use internal activations on a classification task with fixed labels (not ROUGE). (3) Use the HaluEval dataset directly (pre-labeled correct/hallucinated pairs) rather than generating responses. Option 3 is fastest: load Mamba hidden states on HaluEval QA pairs directly (already done in Exp 08/09), bypassing the generation+ROUGE labeling failure.
failure_analysis.hidden_states_are_valid: Despite labeling failure, 04_mamba_hidden_states.npz contains valid hidden states extracted on TruthfulQA input prompts. These are used in Exp 09 (spectral analysis, no labels needed). For hallucination detection, new labels from HaluEval are needed.
